statement.markdown: Write a method to add an element to the end of a queue. The queue is represented as an array. You may not use any built-in queue data structures.
statement.inputFormat: queue: array of integers, element: integer
statement.notes: Make sure to maintain the order of elements in the queue.

examples.explanation: Adding element 4 to the end of the queue
Adding element 5 to the end of the queue

hints.text: Think about how you would manually add an element to the end of a queue.

editorial.title: Broken Queue Enqueue Method
editorial.intuition: You can use the last index of the array to determine the next position for adding an element
editorial.optimalSolution: You can find the last index of the array and add 1 to it to get the next position
commonMistakes: Not updating the capacity of the array when it's full

functionSignature.python: def enqueue(self, queue: list[int], element: int) -> list[int]:

solutions.python:
from typing import List

class Solution:
    def enqueue(self, queue: List[int], element: int) -> List[int]:
        return queue + [element]


# Read from sys.stdin
import sys

def read_array(n):
    return [int(x) for x in sys.stdin.readline().split()]

c = int(sys.stdin.readline())

result = Solution().enqueue(read_array(c), int(sys.stdin.readline()))

# Print to sys.stdout
print(' '.join(map(str, result)))
